title: quercus faginea f. brevicupulata
intro: Quercus faginea f. brevicupulata is a plant species of the Fagaceae family, commonly known as the Breckland oak or Pyrenean oak, characterized by its small leaves and short cupules. This plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly in its bark and leaves, which contain flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Quercus faginea f. brevicupulata include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to support immune function, reduce stress, and promote overall well-being.
